title: The Compile-Run-Fix Loop
concept: Writing code, running it in the sandbox, capturing compiler or runtime errors, and prompting the agent to fix them.

exercises.prompt: What does a compiler do in programming?

options.text: It runs the code to check for errors.
It translates code into machine language.

exercises.explain: A compiler translates your code into a language the computer can understand, which is crucial for running programs.

cards.title: What is the Compile-Run-Fix Loop?
cards.body: The Compile-Run-Fix Loop is a way to write and test your code. First, you write your code, then you run it to see if it works, and if there are errors, you fix them.

cards.title: Compile
cards.body: To compile is to convert your written code into a form that the computer can execute. This helps catch mistakes before running the program.

cards.title: Finding Mistakes
cards.body: Think of compiling as proofreading a book. Just like you catch typos and grammar mistakes before printing, compiling helps find code errors before running it.

cards.title: Example of the Loop
cards.body: 1. Write code to add two numbers. 2. Compile the code to check for errors. 3. If it runs correctly, great! If not, fix the mistakes and compile again.

cards.title: Myth: Compilers Fix Errors Automatically
cards.body: Compilers help find errors, but they don't fix them for you. You have to read the error messages and correct the code yourself.

cards.title: Visualizing the Loop
cards.body: Imagine cycling through a process: write code, test it, and fix issues.

exercises.prompt: What is the first step in the Compile-Run-Fix Loop?

options.text: Fix the code based on errors.
Run the program to test it.
Write the code you want to run.
Look for mistakes in existing code.

exercises.explain: The first step is always to write the code you want to test and run.

exercises.prompt: A compiler only checks your code for syntax errors and does not help with logical errors. True or False?
exercises.explain: This is true; compilers catch syntax errors but won't find mistakes in the logic of your code.

exercises.prompt: The Compile-Run-Fix Loop helps you ___ errors before executing your code.
exercises.sentence: The Compile-Run-Fix Loop helps you capture errors before executing your code.
exercises.explain: The correct word describes what you do with errors in the Compile-Run-Fix Loop.

exercises.prompt: Which step comes after running your code for the first time?

options.text: You should compile the code again.
You should fix any errors found.
You should write new code immediately.

exercises.explain: After running your code, fixing errors is essential to improve your program.

exercises.prompt: In the Compile-Run-Fix Loop, which order of steps is correct?

options.text: Write code, run it, fix errors, run again.
Run it, write code, fix errors, run it.
Fix errors, write code, run it, fix errors.
Run it, fix errors, write code, run again.

exercises.explain: The correct order is to write code first, then run it to check for errors, and fix them as needed. This is the essence of the Compile-Run-Fix Loop!
